title: standardize stop-with-log behavior for blocking conditions
goal: 軽量2AIオーケストレーターが blocking issue を検知した際に、勝手に修正や scope 拡張を行わず、理由・観測事実・提案を再利用可能な標準ログとして保存して停止できるようにする。
scope: tests for deterministic stop record output
constraints: do not implement any automatic fix behavior in this session
when a blocking issue is detected, the system must stop and emit a standardized log record
proposal content must be recorded as reference_only and must not be auto-applied
do not widen scope based on convenience or optimization
changed files must remain within 5 files
if existing stop behavior conflicts with current stabilization rules, prefer explicit stop-with-log and do not broaden repair behavior
objective: Standardize reusable stop logs so the orchestrator can halt safely and explain why without performing unauthorized fixes.
inputs: existing error and report output behavior
outputs: session report proving deterministic stop logging behavior
allowed_changes_detail: orchestration/run_session.py: add minimal helper logic to build and persist a standardized stop decision record for blocking conditions only
orchestration/run_session.py: modify existing blocking-stop path only to call the standardized stop decision logger
backend/tests/test_stop_with_log_standardization.py: add tests for spec_missing, scope_violation, code_state, and verification_only blocking-stop records only
docs/reports/stop_decision_schema_reference.json: add a reference example of the stop decision record schema only

completion_criteria.condition: A standardized stop decision record format exists and includes stop_reason, observed_facts, reference_only_proposals, and next_action.
When spec_missing is detected, the orchestrator stops and persists the standardized stop decision record without applying fixes.
When scope_violation or code-state issue is detected, the orchestrator stops and persists the standardized stop decision record without widening scope.
Successful execution paths remain unaffected for the tested scope after stop-with-log standardization is introduced.
The session adds logging standardization only and does not introduce provider changes, retry changes, or automatic remediation behavior.
Reference proposals in stop logs are explicitly marked as reference_only and are not used as executable instructions.

acceptance_criteria.description: spec_missing produces a standardized stop decision record with reusable evidence
scope_violation produces a standardized stop decision record and does not proceed to automatic repair
code-state issue produces a standardized stop decision record and includes observed facts
reference proposals in stop decision records are explicitly marked as reference_only
successful execution path remains unaffected by stop-with-log standardization for the covered scope
verification-only blocking issue stops with standardized log and does not modify source code

review_points: 副作用なし（既存破壊なし）
